questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Some politicians have expressed apprehensions over the government's proposal to allow a wage hike for the employees of PSUs. They think that such a hike will increase the prices of PSUs. Courses of action: I. The government should abandon the proposal. II. The government should consult and convince those politicians and enact its proposal. III. The government should benefit the employees of the loss-making PSUs and listen to their plea.

choices.e: All of either I or II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The economic scenario of the country is improving but not at the pace it was seen to be going from the previous year. The President rule should be imposed in Bihar. Courses of action: I. Heavy privatisation should be pursued in all states. II. The governments of each of such states should be asked to re-evaluate their economic planning.

choices.d: II and either I or II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: A result of the efforts of some Pakistani scientists and a coalition of some Indian scientists, vital secrets of ISRO are feared to have been leaked to Pakistan. Courses of action: I. The security arrangement around the ISRO should be tightened. II. The Indian scientists involved in the scandal should be brought to court. III. India should approach Pakistan and diplomatically ask her to return the secrets.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Indian press is sceptic about the government's plans to open Indian newsmarket to international competition. It is feared that there are willing biased opinions into Indian media. Courses of action: I. Indian Press should be convinced that the apprehensions are baseless. II. Western press should not be allowed to come to India. III. The government should set up a censor board for Western press.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Despite its various measures, the SEBI has not had much success in controlling the stock market. The brokers continue to flaunt rules making use of the loopholes therein. Courses of action: I. The SEBI should be wound up. II. The rules should be reviewed so that there are no loopholes in them. III. The SEBI should be given more powers so that it could deal sternly with the erring brokers.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: An IAS officer has been killed in Bihar by irate mobs. It is being suspected that the police played a deliberate part in the officer during the incident. Courses of action: I. All the IAS officers in Bihar should be given tight security. II. The police officials concerned should be suspended. III. The Chief Minister of Bihar should resign on moral grounds.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The new wave of the parallel cinema has become a sick industry. Plagued by lack of funds and dwindling talent, it has lost its appeal, which is an unfortunate development. Courses of action: I. The government should provide financial aid to parallel cinema. II. The parallel cinema should change itself to have commercial appeal. III. People should be persuaded to watch parallel cinema.

choices.c: I and either II or III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The pollution level in some parts of Delhi has reached an alarming level. Already, one day in Delhi means an involuntary smoking of 12 cigarettes. Courses of action: I. Automobiles exceeding the pollution limit should be dealt with severely. II. The government should close down some of the high-pollution industries in Delhi. III. Cigarettes should be rationed in Delhi.
